# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

# frozen_string_literal: true

class Admin::ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :require_admin!

  def create
    sql = params.require(:query).to_s
    result = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(sql)
    render json: format_result(result)
  end

  private

  def require_admin!
    head :forbidden unless current_user&.admin?
  end

  def format_result(result)
    if result.respond_to?(:to_a)
      result.to_a
    elsif result.respond_to?(:fields) && result.respond_to?(:values)
      fields = result.fields
      result.values.map { |row| fields.zip(row).to_h }
    else
      result
    end
  end
end
